stem: Triangle PQR is similar to triangle STU, where angle P corresponds to angle S, and angles R and U are right angles. If the cosine of angle Q equals 2 over 15, what is the value of the cosine of angle T?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. Because the triangles are similar, their corresponding acute angles are congruent. Angle Q corresponds to angle T, so the cosine of angle T equals the cosine of angle Q, which is 2 over 15.

Choice A is incorrect: 1 over 15 has no relation to the given cosine.

Choice B is incorrect: 15 over 2 is the reciprocal of the given cosine.

Choice C is incorrect: 13 over 15 is approximately the sine of angle Q.
